meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Git & AI to Solve Coding Blocks | Advanced Guide
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to integrate Git with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster, build better, and ship your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about staring at screens; it's about systematic isolation. By leveraging Git to snapshot your state and AI to perform root-cause analysis, you turn 'I can't debug this' into a deterministic workflow. Use Antigravity for architectural context and Claude Code for iterative, context-aware command line resolution.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of cryptic tracebacks, the cursor blinking like a countdown clock. You’ve changed three lines, broke four features, and the mental map of your codebase has dissolved into chaos. This is the 'Wall of Despair'—the exact point where most students quit. But here is the secret: professional engineers don't debug by memory; they debug through systematic state management.

By integrating Git as your source of truth and AI as your tactical analyst, you transform the debugging process from a guessing game into a surgical operation. We are moving beyond simple copy-pasting; we are building a closed-loop system where your version control history feeds the intelligence of your AI agents, allowing you to isolate, reproduce, and resolve errors with surgical precision.

steps.title: Establish the Debugging Sandbox
steps.description: Before changing code, use Git to create a clean state. This prevents 'ghost bugs' where your fixes introduce new, unrelated issues.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and generate a set of unit tests for the specific module where the bug occurs. Ensure these tests fail in the current state.
steps.claude_code_command: git checkout -b fix/debug-session && claude code 'Create a reproduction script for the current error'

steps.title: Contextual Traceback Analysis
steps.description: Feed the stack trace into your AI agent to identify the exact line of failure rather than guessing the source.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine the following stack trace and cross-reference it with the logic in my main controller. Identify the root cause of the null reference.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code 'Analyze the latest error log in /logs/error.log and explain why it is failing'

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Failure
steps.description: Use Git diffs to see exactly what changed since the last stable build, letting the AI pinpoint the regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the current file state with the last stable commit. Identify which logic change introduced the regression in the API handler.
steps.claude_code_command: git diff HEAD~1 | claude code 'Review this diff and explain the impact on the current runtime'

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Generate a fix based on the isolation findings and verify it immediately within the sandbox.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a code patch for the identified bug that adheres to our project's existing design patterns and security standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code 'Implement the suggested fix and run the test suite to confirm success'

steps.title: Commit and Verify Workflow
steps.description: Once the test passes, commit the fix with a meaningful message to keep your history clean for future debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the technical changes made to resolve this bug into a professional Git commit message.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'fix: resolve runtime exception in user authentication module' && git push

pro_tips: Always commit before asking the AI for a fix; it allows you to 'git reset --hard' if the AI takes you down a rabbit hole.
Use 'git bisect' alongside your AI agent to find the exact commit that introduced a bug in a large codebase.
Never paste your entire project into the AI; use Git diffs to provide only the relevant context to stay within token limits.
Treat your Git commit messages as documentation for your future self; include the 'Why' of the fix, not just the 'What'.
Configure your .gitignore to exclude AI-generated temporary files to keep your repo clean and performance-optimized.
content.next_steps: Now that you've mastered the debugging loop, apply this to a new feature branch. Practice intentional breaking of your code to see if your new AI-Git workflow can catch it in under 5 minutes.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to stop guessing and start building. Don't let a bug stop your momentum; make it a part of your learning cycle. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
